user_query:
I need a deep Swift code review across concurrency, error handling, memory management, and SwiftUI observation patterns for our app. Please read all source snippets under input/ (they are Swift code stored as .txt files):
- input/NetworkManager.swift.txt
- input/Store.swift.txt
- input/ViewModels.swift.txt
- input/ViewController.swift.txt
- input/Parser.swift.txt

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/review.md — A comprehensive narrative review that includes:
   - Overview
   - Files Reviewed (list each input file)
   - Checklist (explicitly cover the 11 items from the Swift review checklist: force unwraps on runtime data, weak capture in stored closures, weak delegate properties, async let/TaskGroup for independent async ops, Task cancellation checks, actors have mutable state to protect, Sendable correctness, explicit error handling (no empty catch), LocalizedError conformance for custom errors, nested @Observable usage, @Bindable for two-way bindings)
   - Findings (numbered, with Severity and file:line references)
   - Recommendations with concrete code examples (before/after)
   - References (cite concurrency.md, error-handling.md, common-mistakes.md, observable.md by name)
   Ensure you discuss: async/await patterns, actor reentrancy, Sendable, try/try?/try!, LocalizedError, retain cycles and weak delegates, force unwraps, and SwiftUI @Observable/@Bindable usage. Include at least one recommendation to use async let where appropriate.
2) output/risk_summary.json — A machine-checkable metrics summary with these keys and integer counts (derive from actual code you reviewed):
   {
     "try_bang_count": number,                      // occurrences of "try!"
     "force_unwrap_count": number,                 // occurrences of unsafe runtime force unwraps like x! or foo()! (exclude "!=" and "try!")
     "iuo_declarations": number,                   // declarations using implicitly unwrapped optionals like ": Type!"
     "empty_catch_blocks": number,                 // catch { } blocks with no handling other than whitespace/comments
     "weak_delegate_missing": number,              // delegate properties declared without weak
     "retain_cycle_closure_suspects": number,      // stored closures capturing self without [weak self]
     "sequential_await_opportunities": number,     // places where async let/TaskGroup could be used
     "actor_reentrancy_risks": number,             // actor methods mutating state after an await based on pre-await checks
     "missing_sendable_or_unchecked": number,      // cross-actor/shared types used without Sendable or with @unchecked without synchronization
     "observation_issues": number                  // @Observable/@Bindable/@State misuse instances
   }
   Include a top-level "files" object mapping each input filename to an object with per-file counts for the same keys.
3) output/checklist.csv — CSV with columns: item,status,notes. Include one row for each of the 11 checklist items above. status should be one of PASS|FAIL|N/A. notes should be concise and reference affected files/lines.
4) output/patches.jsonl — One JSON object per line proposing concrete fixes with this schema for at least 8 distinct issues across the codebase:
   {"file":"<relative input path>","line":<number>,"issue":"<short title>","before":"<exact code excerpt>","after":"<proposed replacement>","rationale":"<why this fixes it>"}

Be precise about line numbers and include code snippets in the review. No placeholders. Use conservative, production-safe recommendations. If you suggest using @unchecked Sendable, justify synchronization. Where errors are wrapped, ensure LocalizedError provides user-facing descriptions. Where closures are stored or long-lived, verify [weak self] and cancellation checks. In SwiftUI, verify @Bindable and nested @Observable usage and note any @ObservationIgnored needs.
